content.problemStatement:
You are given an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN.

We define f(i,j)=f(i, j) =f(i,j)= number of corresponding bits in binary representation of AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ which are different. For example, the array  A=[6,10,15A=[6, 10, 15A=[6,10,15] then f(2,3)=2f(2, 3) = 2f(2,3)=2 since binary representation of A2A_2A2​ and A3A_3A3​ are 101010101010 and 111111111111. The second and the fourth bit differ, so f(2,3)=2f(2, 3) = 2f(2,3)=2.

Find the value of ∑i=1N∑j=iNf(i,j)\sum_{i=1}^{N} \sum_{j=i}^{N} f(i, j)∑i=1N​∑j=iN​f(i,j).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value of ∑i=1N∑j=iNf(i,j)\sum_{i=1}^{N} \sum_{j=i}^{N} f(i, j)∑i=1N​∑j=iN​f(i,j).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
